steps.caption_md: Finding the middle of `1 -> 2 -> 3 -> 4 -> 5`. Both `slow` and `fast` start at the head, value **1**.
One loop iteration: `slow` moved one step to **2**, `fast` moved two steps to **3**.
Another iteration: `slow` is now at **3**, `fast` is at **5** — the last node. `fast.next` is `None`. What does the loop do next?

predict.prompt_md: `fast` sits on **5** (the last node) so `fast.next` is `None`. What happens on the next check of the loop condition?

choices.label_md: Stop — `fast.next` is `None` so the loop condition fails; `slow` (3) is already the middle.
Take one more double step, since `fast` itself is not `None`.
Reset `slow` back to the head and start over.

predict.explanation_md: The guard is `while fast and fast.next`. Since `fast.next` is `None`, the loop halts immediately — `slow` has already covered exactly half the distance and correctly points at node **3**.

steps.caption_md: Loop ends. `slow` holds **3**, the middle node of this 5-node list — found in a single pass, with only two pointer variables.
